name: Deep Dive Workshop - Slow social evening for curious minds
description:
Deep Dive is a slow social evening for curious minds — designed for people who miss exploring things deeply.
In a world of shallow scrolling and fast content, this gathering creates space to slow down, follow your curiosity, and spend an evening immersed in meaningful exploration inside a cozy bookstore café.
Participants choose a topic that genuinely excites them and spend the evening following their curiosity through guided prompts, reading, research, conversations, reflection, and unexpected rabbit holes. For those unsure of what to explore, curated prompts and a surprise topic bowl will help spark curiosity.
It could be anything from quantum mechanics, matcha, mythology, urban loneliness, and dreams to typography, dinosaurs, architecture, cinema, rituals, space, or human behaviour.
The evening concludes when participants share interesting discoveries, surprising insights, questions, and reflections from their exploration journey — creating space for thoughtful conversations instead of performative presentations.
This is not a lecture, networking event, or academic workshop.
It is a cozy, curiosity-led evening for people who enjoy learning, wondering, discussing ideas, and experiencing the joy of intellectual exploration together.
No expertise required. Just curiosity.
What's included

Toughtfully designed prompts and exploration frameworks


A slow, intentional social learning space with fellow curious minds


Short eats and beverages during the experience

name: Handmade Guitars: Passion in the Art & Craft of Lutherie - A talk
description:
Lutherie is the making of stringed instruments out of wood, by hand.
290-yr old Mahogany that no one may see anymore. Trees that have, through many decades of struggle, created unimaginably beautiful patterns of wood grain in their own bodies. 
Most of us are used to seeing factory-made instruments and tend to take them for granted. Understanding Lutherie gives us a glimpse into a very different world - one that combines design, deep understanding of tone and timbre, and the manner in which every part of a guitar affects them, extreme respect for details and more details, the love of wood and mastery over the craftsmanship of wood - a world of limitless possibilities, where no two instruments are ever alike.
This is the story of a pair of luthier-made instruments, a Bass and a Guitar; the passion and love that created them, and the extraordinary relationship between Luthier and Musician they are based upon.
Vinoo has been playing music for longer than anyone ought to, and has played rock, jazz, blues, fusion, and music that no one listens to. He is a firm believer in musical promiscuity and plays/has played bass with the Rex Rozario Quintet, Aftermath, River, Gerard Machado Network, Ministry of Blues, Cantonment Jazz Terminus, Chronic Blues Band, Bangalore Jazz & Abstract Music Club, Southern Discomfort, etc., ad nauseum.
He also plays lead guitar with the underground group, the Sarjapur Blues Band, proving Frank Zappa’s statement: "All bass players are failed lead guitarists."

name: ALT EFF Film Club x Atta Gallata - Join us for a screening of Miles Away
description:
Join us for a screening of Miles Away; a visual and auditory immersion of the lives of migrant, female brick labors. This film is intimate, cinematic and sensory.
Part of our 2024 Program, this was Juror Dia Mirza's reaction:
"I was very moved by 'Miles Away' as for the first time I saw the perspective of what Climate Change is doing to women. It was very special to see so many women coming together to tell the story and feeling at ease as they shared personal moments with the female filmmakers. I felt as if I was a part of their lives."
To become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date. All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.
What's Included?
Exclusive screenings of the world's best environmental cinema
Access to a community of environmentally inclined, film-loving people
A chance to express yourself at ALT EFF Voices
Post-film discussions.
Student Discount
50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).
CODE: ALTSTUDENT
ABOUT ALT EFF Film Club
If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.
It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.

name: A Pichwai Art Workshop - Workshop
description:
Immerse yourself in the timeless beauty of Pichwai art and create your own handcrafted wall décor. In this beginner-friendly workshop, participants will paint a beautifully pre-marked MDF wall décor base while exploring the rich heritage, motifs, and artistic traditions of Pichwai painting.Guided step-by-step, you will learn essential painting techniques, colour application, detailing, and decorative elements that bring this traditional art form to life. Whether you are a complete beginner or an art enthusiast, this workshop is designed to be relaxing, enjoyable, and accessible to all skill levels.All materials will be provided, and participants will take home a finished artwork ready to display in their home or creative space.
Workshop Highlights:
• Introduction to the history and motifs of Pichwai art 
• Beginner friendly & Step-by-step guided painting session 
• 10-inch Pre-marked MDF wall décor base provided 
• All materials included 
• Take home your completed handcrafted artwork

name: Hitched Or Ditched - Book launch
description:
✨ An Afternoon of Honest Conversation on Love & Relationships ✨
Join us at Atta Galatta for the launch of Hitched or Ditched—a thought-provoking exploration of modern relationships, love, heartbreak, healing, and self-discovery.
In a candid fireside conversation, Love Wizard Paromita Bannerjee and Wisdom Activator Rahul Singh will share insights from the book along with the experience of writing it, followed by an interactive audience discussion.
Whether you are navigating a relationship, recovering from one, questioning beliefs about love, or simply curious about the matters of heart, this afternoon promises meaningful conversations and fresh perspectives.
💫 Expect:
• A lively fireside chat
• Reflections on love, relationships, and personal growth
• Audience Q&A and interaction
• Book signing and networking

name: The Invisible Lines - Book launch
description:
Step into the 80s and 90s with a good dose of Dr Rajkumar songs and hot cup of filter kaapi.
Join us for the book launch of The Invisible Lines by Sudha Subramanian, and celebrate story telling, writing and writing community.

name: Nature Journaling Workshop: Leaves, Flowers & Seed Pods - Watercolour Workshop
description:
Slow down, look closer, and discover the beauty hidden in everyday nature with me @https://www.instagram.com/watercolorbycharu?igsh=NjEzbmV6djU1ZX By  ( watercolorbycharu)
Join us for a beginner-friendly Nature Journaling Workshop where we'll explore leaves, flowers, and seed pods through observation, sketching, watercolor, and mindful note-taking. No prior drawing experience is needed—only curiosity and a willingness to notice.
Nature journaling is not about creating perfect artwork. It is about paying attention, asking questions, documenting discoveries, and being fully present in the moment.
 
Together, we will:
 • Observe natural objects closely using all our senses
• Learn simple techniques for sketching and watercolor journaling
• Document shapes, colours, textures, patterns, and personal observations
• Create a beautiful journal page that combines art, science, and storytelling
 
Whether you're  a nature lover, educator, student, or someone looking for a mindful creative practice, this workshop offers a gentle and inspiring introduction to nature journaling.

name: Dum Dama Dum - Children’s Theatre and Reading Festival
description:
The Theatre and Reading festival Dum Dama Dum by Atta Galatta that began in 2017 unites two beautiful things in a child’s life, the love for drama and love for reading. The festival will have plays for children and families from various renowned theatre groups while at the bookstore, they can enter into a world of books from their favourite authors and publishers.
The Day Pass includes access to all 3 scheduled plays.
Links to purchase tickets for individual plays are below.
Adults and children require tickets.
Schedule:
3:00 PM- Oh Mama, Where Water Thou?- presented by Theatre On Your Own
https://urbanaut.app/spot/oh-mama-where-water-thou 
4:30 PM- The Paper Thieves- presented by TheatreSHIFT
https://urbanaut.app/spot/the-paper-thieves
6:00 PM- Scandal in Fairyland- presented by Urban Chaupaal
https://urbanaut.app/spot/scandal-in-fairyland

name: Authentic Relating Games - Communication Workshop
description:
Ever wonder what is possible when we drop the small talk and connect for real?

Authentic Relating Games is a global practice of guided, playful yet deep exercises (the 'games') designed to help people connect more honestly and meaningfully — whether with close friends or complete strangers.

Through structured partner and group conversations, you will get to practice speaking from the heart, deep listening, and noticing what's alive in you as you relate to others.  The games create space for more honesty, curiosity, and real human connection — often in ways that feel surprisingly fun, refreshing, and profoundly moving.

What to Expect

- A Structured Container:  We will start as a full group to set some simple guidelines that ensure the space feels safe, respectful, and confidential.
- Activities with Partners, Trios, and the Large Group: We'll keep changing partners throughout the evening (no need to bring one!).
- Guided Prompts and Demos: We'll guide you step-by-step through the games and provide live demonstrations so you know what to do.
- Total Autonomy: You are always in the driver's seat.  You choose how deeply you want to go, or pass on a prompt or game at any time.

No experience needed.  Come as you are.  If you've never done anything like this before, don't worry — it's simple and there is no pressure to get it "right".

About the facilitator:
Roy Jacob is the founder of the Conscious Communication Academy and has been facilitating workshops and retreats for over 15 years, mostly for changemakers as well as team retreats for NGOs and companies.  Roy grew up in the U.S. before finding his calling and coming to India (where his roots are) in 2003.  He believes in the transformative power of combining radical honesty with radical care.

name: WhatsApp for Business - Workshop
description:
First Brick Collective is hosting a practical workshop with Gaurav Goel, Founder of Timeboard, on mastering WhatsApp for Business. He has worked with over 200 businesses and will be breaking down the steps for building an active, loyal community straight through direct chat.
Just strategies that save you time and protect your bottom line.
 
Limited seats.

name: Divine Delicacies - Storytelling and Menu Tasting Session
description: Divine Delicacies offers a sensory journey through India's sacred kitchens. Join storyteller Aparna Jaishankar to uncover the myths, history, and folklore behind beloved temple prasadams. This immersive session blends captivating narratives with a menu tasting, offering a unique opportunity to connect with culinary heritage and faith. An experience for the heart and palate!

name: Paint, Pull, Repeat - Serigraphy workshop
description: Join Vaishali for a hands-on introduction to screen printing, where you'll learn a simple and accessible method of creating your own prints using embroidery hoops and screen mesh. Design, print, and take home a unique pencil pouch while exploring the basics of this versatile printmaking technique. No prior experience required—just bring your curiosity and creativity!

name: Thought Bubbles of Becoming - Book launch
description:
In a rapidly changing world shaped by technology, uncertainty, and constant acceleration, Thought Bubbles of Becoming invites readers to pause and reflect on what it means to remain human.Through a collection of personal reflections and poems, Dr. Sonali Malhotra explores themes of learning, leadership, identity, relationships, curiosity, emotional depth, and the lifelong process of becoming. Drawing from experiences in education and life, the book offers thoughtful insights for anyone navigating change, responsibility, and personal growth.
 
This is not a book of arrival.
This is a book about becoming.
 
"When machines learned to answer, teachers became guardians of humanity."

name: Postcard to My Future Self - A Mindful Self-Reflection & Connection Social
description:
When was the last time you took a moment to slow down and speak honestly to yourself? Not about your to-do list, but about your growth, the weights you are carrying, and the hopes you want to see bloom?
 
On Sunday, join Team Pause and Breathe for a unique, deeply intentional evening designed to foster connection, introspection, and mindful presence. In a warm and welcoming space, you will engage in a guided activity centered on writing a postcard to your future self. Together, we will reflect, practice the art of intentional active listening, and try to show up as our authentic selves to a room full of soon-to-be friends.
 
Come prepared to pause, breathe, and leave behind a message that your future self will thank you for. Come exactly as you are.

name: Sharing Circle - Sharing Session
description:
Every year, Dhaara brings together people from different walks of life to celebrate and reconnect with nature through trees, conversations, storytelling, workshops and community-driven experiences. This year, the festival will take place on Sunday, 28th June 2026, under the theme Rising from the Roots — a reminder that all growth begins from being deeply rooted, much like trees themselves.
In the lead-up to the festival, we are hosting a series of community activations across weekends, one of which is our Sharing Circle on 21st June around 6pm. Sharing Circle is an intimate and conscious gathering centred around dialogue, storytelling and reflection — creating a safe, grounding and interactive space for conversations around nature, connection and the idea of “roots” in today’s rapidly transforming urban life. This edition will be hosted by Akshata Bhadranna, who leads Green Mic — a platform and community deeply engaged in sustainability & conservation.
Join us for an evening of conversations that bring us the remembrance of roots and help us connect with nature and each other.

name: The Next Curve - Meetup
description:
The Next Curve is a community for people who believe that life can become richer, healthier, and more meaningful with age.
We are on a mission to reinvent ageing for people and advocate for a healthier planet through awareness around regenerative agriculture and conscious living.
At The Next Curve, we explore the intersection of physical health, emotional well-being, mental resilience, purpose, relationships, nutrition, longevity, and environmental responsibility. Through conversations, walks, workshops, expert talks, community gatherings, and shared experiences, we aim to create a space where people support one another in living better while contributing to a better world.
This is not a community defined by age.
It is a community defined by mindset.
Whether you are 25 or 75, if you care about your health, your future, and the future of the planet, you belong here.
Our Belief
Because Healthy People Need a Healthy Planet.
The Next Curve
Growing Older. Living Better. Becoming More. 🌿

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Food is more than sustenance; it is a map of our memories and a bridge to different cultures. This  month, we continue our dialogue on the narratives that connect what we read to what we eat as we follow the story of a Japanese novelist and her interpreter as they eat their way through colonial Taiwan.
Book of the month: Taiwan Travelogue written Yáng Shuāng-zǐ  translated by Lin King
When: Saturday, 27th June, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots (link in bio)
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a 
community for people who to love to read and eat

name: Paper and Pride- A DIY Pocket Magazine Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join us for a messy, creative evening of making a handmade mini-magazine from scratch. We are filling pages with everything that makes us who we are: identity, pride, culture, and hard-learned lessons.You design page by page. We bind them together. Everyone takes a copy of the finished mini magazine home. Real. Unfiltered. 
Made by actual humans. We're making one together - collaborative pages around identity, pride (literal, cultural, professional, queer, all of it), what you stand for, things you're learning the hard way. It's messy. It's supposed to be. By the end, we have a real handmade zine that everyone contributed to. You take it home. You flip through it later and remember this night. 
This is open to LGBT+ community and allies. Everyone. Because pride isn't one thing.

name: Neeraj Pandey's Goodboy - Dramatised storytelling
description:
A 70 mins dramatised storytelling show written and performed by the lyricist, screenwriter and Author Neeraj Pandey.

Synopsis: Two cousins who were once inseparable haven’t spoken in ten years. A phone call about one’s hospitalisation forces the other to
make the visit. Would they be able to look beyond what broke between them?

name: Tinsy Doo Tells A Tale - Storytelling & Book Signing
description:
Join author Aarushi Jain for a delightful storytelling session featuring a story from her children’s book - Tiny Tales with Tinsy Doo.
An engaging experience filled with imagination, creativity, and fun for children and parents, followed by an exclusive book signing session.
Age: 4-8 yrs

name: Funny Games - Film Screening
description: A family's vacation at a secluded lakeside home takes an unsettling turn following the arrival of two young strangers. What begins as a seemingly ordinary encounter gradually erodes the family's sense of security, creating an atmosphere of mounting tension. Through this premise, the film explores themes of control, spectatorship, and the audience's relationship with cinematic violence. The screening will be followed by a moderated discussion examining the film through the lens of meta-cinema.

name: Bangalore Finance Club Presents: The Creator Economy - Content, Capital, and The Economics of Being an Influencer
description:
Is content creation just a hobby, or is it the ultimate modern asset class?
For the Bangalore Finance Club’s 3rd event, we are diving deep into the economics of content creation. Join us as we sit down with Prateek Thakker, one of Bangalore’s pioneer food and lifestyle creators who successfully built a powerhouse brand from scratch, commanding a community of over 100k followers on Instagram.
Forget the surface-level glamour—we are getting into the real numbers. Prateek will share his journey of building a brand from the ground up, navigating the unspoken hurdles of the industry, and seizing the massive opportunities that open up once you crack the algorithm.
Most importantly, we are breaking down the money math. From monetization structures and brand deal dynamics to treating influence as genuine equity, this session will dissect the true unit economics of the creator class. Whether you are an aspiring creator, an entreprenuer hoping to hire influencers to build your brand or just someone interested about a content creator's life, this is a blueprint you cannot afford to miss.
Networking opportunities will follow the event.

name: Feel. Experience. Design. - Design thinking workshop
description:
Design Thinking is a human-centred approach to problem-solving - one that starts not with solutions, but with genuine understanding of the people the problem affects. Originally developed in the world of product and service design, it is now used across industries, organisations, and social contexts to tackle everything from workplace culture to public policy.

The workshop takes participants through all five stages of Design Thinking - Empathise, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test - using the tools of Applied Theatre. Every stage is experienced in the body, not just understood in the mind.

Who is this for?
Professionals, practitioners, and curious thinkers who work with people, systems, or ideas - and want a deeper, more honest way of understanding the problems they solve. Useful for product managers, HR and L&D professionals, designers, consultants, educators, social sector workers, facilitators, and anyone who has sat through one too many sticky-note sessions and wanted something more real.

No prior knowledge of Design Thinking or theatre required. Curiosity is the only prerequisite.

About the Facilitator
Bejoy Balagopal - Co-founder of First Drop Theatre. Part engineer, part storyteller, long-time theatre practitioner. 25 years navigating corporate and creative worlds. Holds a Professional Certificate in Design Thinking and Innovation with AI from IIM Kozhikode.
Poster Description - Features a warm, textured background with soft circular shapes. The words - "Feel. Experience. Design." - are displayed in large, hand-lettered text, with a photograph of the facilitator in the upper right. A row of small sticky notes are scattered across the middle of the poster, each carrying a single word - Feel, Inhabit, Question, Build, Break, Discover.

name: Natural Dyeing Workshop for Beginners - Art Workshop
description:
Discover the fascinating world of natural colours in this hands-on Natural Dyeing Workshop designed for beginners. Learn how everyday natural materials such as onion peels, marigold flowers, sappan wood, indigo, and other plant-based ingredients can be transformed into beautiful, sustainable dyes for textiles.
The workshop will guide you through the complete dyeing process, starting with an introduction to natural dyes and the importance of fabric preparation. You will learn the fundamentals of mordanting : a crucial step that helps colours bond with the fabric and improves colour fastness. Through step-by-step demonstrations, participants will explore dye extraction, fabric preparation, dyeing techniques, and colour development using a range of natural ingredients.
The session will also introduce dye modification techniques, showing how simple modifiers can alter and expand the colour palette obtained from a single dye source. Participants will gain practical knowledge of sustainable textile practices while experimenting with colours and patterns.
As part of the workshop, you will create your own tie-dyed naturally dyed fabric samples and take them home, along with the confidence to continue exploring natural dyeing techniques on your own.
Duration: 2 hours Suitable for: Beginners, craft enthusiasts, students, designers, and anyone interested in sustainable textile practices.

name: Move through Math  - Book launch
description:
This event marks the release of Move Through Math, a handbook designed for parents, facilitators, and educators working with children aged 5–10. Rooted in the principles of embodied education, the book explores how abstract mathematical concepts can be understood through movement, sound, rhythm, and spatial awareness. By adapting movements from Bharatanatyam, the handbook offers a culturally grounded, multisensory approach to STEM learning.
Program Highlights:


Author Presentation: An overview of the educational framework and the philosophy of embodied pedagogy.


Live Demonstration: Selected mathematical concepts expressed through Bharatanatyam-inspired movement.


Interactive Segment: A guided, accessible movement exploration for the audience.


Q&A and Networking: Open discussion followed by a book signing.

name: Plaeto Shoe Painting Workshop - Painting workshop by Plaeto
description:
Plaeto's Sole Art Junior is a creative workshop designed for young artists and their parents to explore art together. Through hands-on activities, kids unleash their imagination and craft unique designs in a warm, playful setting. A celebration of creativity, self-expression, and the joy of making something truly one's own.
Age Group: 04-14 years

name: Fat2Fit – Intimate Reading Session - Book Reading
description:
Over five years ago, I stood in front of a mirror at 110 kilos and made a promise to myself. That promise changed my life.
Fat2Fit is not a book about weight loss. It is a story about resilience, self-respect, healing, second chances, and discovering what becomes possible when we refuse to give up on ourselves.
I would love for you to join me for an intimate reading and conversation as I share stories from the book, the lessons behind the journey, and reflections that never made it onto social media.

name: Ghost of Garrincha - Paperclip Live - Storytelling
description:
The Paperclip is an independent media platform widely followed on social media for its deeply researched, authentic storytelling in an era of viral misinformation. Known for turning complex histories into compelling narratives, it now brings its most powerful football stories to a live audience for the first time in the city.
With the FIFA World Cup going on, this show taps into a moment when football is already on everyone’s mind - but goes far beyond the game. It speaks to an urban audience that is globally aware, engaged with geopolitics, and curious about the world beyond headlines. Blending football with power, politics, and human stories, the experience is designed to be highly engaging - even for those who don’t follow the sport. It’s not just a show for fans, but for anyone who enjoys sharp, thought-provoking storytelling.

name: Abstract Art Workshop  - Art Workshop
description:
Hi, I'm Thomas, an artist, content creator, and graphic designer. I'm hosting an art workshop at Atta Galatta on the 11th of July. The workshop will be a 2 hour secession, that's going to be similar to a crash course on the basic as well as the advanced techniques of abstract art.
 
In the workshop, I will teach you some of the important avenues that would help you monetize your artwork as well. You will also understand just how therapeutic and calming this art form really is.
 
Key pointers on pricing, how you could turn your art into merchandise, and how to go about the framing and sales aspects will also be covered during the course of the 2 hours.

name: Unhurried Afternoon - Sip and Paint Experience
description: An Unhurried Afternoon is a beginner-friendly sip-and-paint experience designed for those looking to slow down and spend a creative afternoon away from the rush of everyday life. Over two relaxed hours, participants will create a cozy café-inspired acrylic painting featuring a sunflower and blue coffee mug while enjoying a beverage and good company. No prior painting experience is required. All materials are provided, and participants will be guided step-by-step through the process. Ticket includes painting materials and one beverage
